etymology.description: 马(馬) (mǎ, "horse") + 上(上) (shàng, "on; above; up"); riding on horseback was the fastest mode of transport in ancient times, enabling swift movement, hence the sense of 'immediately' or 'right away'.
etymology.interpretation: The character 马(馬) (mǎ, "horse") represents the speed and mobility of a horse, a symbol of rapid movement. 上(上) (shàng, "on; above; up") suggests the position or action on such a swift vehicle. The combination evokes the speed at which one could move when immediately mounting or being on a horse, illustrating the sense of 'right away' or 'immediately'.

examples.chinese: 我马上到家(我馬上到家)。
examples.english: I will arrive home right away.

examples.chinese: 请马上回复(請馬上回覆)。
事情发生后(事情發生後)，他马上道歉(他馬上道歉)。
examples.pinyin: Shìqíng fāshēng hòu, tā mǎshàng dàoqiàn.
examples.english: After the event happened, he apologized immediately.
